title: The Unknown

evidence.note: Published 1927; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Unknown” (renewals ~1954, 1955, 1956)

US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Unknown is a 1927 American silent horror film directed by Tod Browning for Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer, starring Lon Chaney as 'Alonzo the Armless,' a carnival knife-thrower, and a young Joan Crawford as the woman he loves. Widely regarded as the finest of the Browning-Chaney collaborations, it was thought lost until a print was recovered from the Cinematheque Francaise in 1968. As a 1927 US publication, the film print is in the public domain by expiry of the 95-year copyright term; score, story, and restoration layers are left undetermined pending their own evidence.
